observations.recommendation: We recommended that management commence the process of disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and management agreed to make a follow-up with PAO, and PDIC to fast-track the resolution of the case and the filing of appropriate charges against the persons found to be liable. observations.reason: The recommendation was not complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit a valid explanation on the employment of Shopping modality for all procurements below ₱100,000.00 instead of Negotiated Procurement (Small Value Procurement) for procurements outside the scope of Shopping. Also, we recommended Management to submit the supporting documents such as Mayor’s/Business Permit, PhilGEPS Registration, Statement of the Prospective bidder that it is not blacklisted, Sworn affidavit and Omnibus Sworn Statement required in Appendix A of Annex “H” and COA Circular 2012-001 to establish validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Chairperson on March 12, 2021 replied that they failed to perceive the right procurement modality but assured the audit team to adopt the proper mode of procurement on the next forthcoming procurements.  He also vowed to submit the required documents.

The Bids and Awards Committee is instructed to thoroughly recommend the mode of procurement for every purchase amounting to less than ₱100,000.00 and regularly update themselves on matters regarding procurement. The BAC agreed to observe the Revised IRR of the RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Review of CY 2021 transactions, on a test basis, some procurement made through shopping did not meet the prerequisite of such modality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Local Chief Executive ensure optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) and fast-track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as required under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the Updated Guidelines on the Appropriation and Utilization of the 20% of the IRA for Development Projects.
observations.managementAction: The Management provides explanation of the non-implementation of the projects as discussed in the Part II.
observations.reason:
This observation is reiterated in CY 2021.

In CY 2021, the Municipality has unutilized 20% development fund of ₱10,506,897.63 budgeted for capital outlay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to establish clear cut guidelines on the selection and grant of AIFCS which may be aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02, series of 2014.  It is also recommended that an explanation on why travel expenses for Job Order workers were charged to the appropriation for AIFCS.
observations.managementAction: A written reply dated February 17, 2022 in reference to AOM No. 2022-002 (2021), the MSWDO mentioned that it was already discussed with the management regarding the adoption of DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 series of 2014 so that guidelines will be set and only those eligible clients can avail of this program.
observations.reason:
This observation is reiterated in CY 2021.

In CY 2021, we have observed that travelling expenses of Job Order personnel were charged against AICS which is contrary to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 02 series of 2014.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances in their respective records, identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books, and to perform the same regularly pursuant to Section 63 (F) of BLGF-DOF Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM), to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to hasten the physical count of inventory items by type and reported in the RPCI to be submitted to the concerned auditor. Likewise, consider early timing on the conduct of physical count to be able to meet the deadlines.
We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supply Ledger Card and for the Municipal Treasurer to use the prescribed format of the Stock Cards to facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of the accounts periodically.

We recommended that Management instruct the Inventory Committee to hasten the physical count of PPE as of December 31, 2021, render the report thereon, and provide a copy of the report to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. Likewise, consider early conduct of the physical count to be able to meet the deadline.
Also, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and/or Property and Supplier Officer to maintain property cards and the Accounting Office for the Property Ledger Card to facilitate monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to accrue the remaining unpaid balance of the completed project and the corresponding asset account with the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
Construction in Progress – RHB 		18,968,549.30
Accounts Payable				18,968,549.30
To accrue the unpaid portion of PRDP completed project.
Henceforth, observe the proper recording and adjustments of accounts and transactions in accordance with MNGAS, IPSAS and other existing accounting rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately liquidate the financial assistance received with purposes already completed/implemented, and coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the return of the excess funds to the Source Agencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to locate/produce the documents pertaining to the said unidentified items including the journal entries made without proper supporting documents  to come up with appropriate adjusting entries, and make further action thereon, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the Municipal Development Council, to submit an explanation/justification for non-implementation of priority projects for CY 2021 including the previous year’s allotments. We also recommended that Management always ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Submit the required reports on time and with complete signatories in order for the Audit Team to timely perform the audit procedures on the utilization of the fund; and
Initiate the process of recruitment to fill in the mandatory vacant positions so that efficiency and effectiveness in the discharge of the functions can be attained.

We recommended that Management investigate the concerned personnel involved in the implementation of this project to determine accountability for the lapse/error, and to take appropriate action thereon. Henceforth, install adequate control measures to ensure that the site survey and investigation during the preparation of the detailed engineering of Infrastructure Projects are carefully performed to avoid the incurrence of the same lapse.
Also, we recommended that the Management request for funding and authority, through the Sanggunian Bayan, to negotiate for the acquisition of these private lots, following pertinent laws, rules, and regulations so that the intended purpose of the project could still be fully maximized.

We recommended that the Management:
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Refrain from appropriating in the Annual Budget the payment of honoraria intended for the BAC, instead, assess its utilization to determine if there are savings that can supplement the deficiency from collections mentioned above; and
Limit the disbursements within the amount appropriated to avoid incurrence of overdraft;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue recording the proceeds from the sale of bidding documents to Due to Employees and Officers under General Fund and revisit the pertinent rules and regulations including the Revised Chart of Accounts per COA Circular No. 2015-009 for the proper account to be used.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to discontinue recording the transactions related to travel expenses as donations account;
In case the Job Order is required to travel, in relation to the performance of their duty, the travelling expense shall be limited to actual expenditure subject to reimbursements; and
Henceforth, refrain from charging the travelling expenses to the AICS program to avoid depleting the funds.

We recommended that the LDRRMO prepare the required summary/list of donations in-kind received, distributed and balances and submit to the audit team.  The one-time separate report for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submit to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnish the team and accordingly post the required report in the NDRRMC website and agency website.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO complied with the preparation and submission of the required reports to the audit team and the NDRRMC through the OCD.
observations.reason: The summary/list of donations in-kind received from March to December 2020 was prepared and submitted to the team on February 18, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit justification for undertaking the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act without complying with the requirements of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and to be always updated with the issuances of the GPPB as the governing board for all the procurement activities of the government.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC Chairperson explained that because of the need of the LGU to provide its constituents with the basic needs during the pandemic, the required documents, as stated in the GPPB Circular 01-2020 were not properly scrutinized and completely attached. Also, in the exigency of the pandemic, the LGU procured food and medical supplies without delay to cater to the needs of the constituents.

The Bids and Awards Committee have been instructed to be updated of all of the issuances regarding procurement. An explanation/justification letter have already been submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Written reply was received on March 12, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation on the utilization of the grant amounting ₱169,849.62 for purposes other than for the implementation of Covid-19 PPAs/expenses, and the approved Driver’s Trip Tickets amounting ₱100,008.91 to establish the validity of the payments.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) expressed that the trip tickets issued were based on the routinary activities, neglecting the fact that all transactions were done at the height of the COVID-19 pandemic. 

The LGU justified that the transactions were COVID-19 related. The MBO added that the original trip tickets will be updated to reflect covid-related activities.
observations.reason:
Written reply was received on March 12, 2021.

No similar audit observation has been noted in the current year’s audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit explanation/justification on the non-compliance with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020. 

Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid non-compliance in the future.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairperson, in his reply stated that due to the urgency in the procurement during the COVID-19 pandemic, the BAC can hardly cope with the procedures such as posting in the GPPB portal considering the fact that the employees were on work skeletal arrangement. Moreover, there was a difficulty in the access of PhilGEPS and GPPB portal because of slow internet connection. The procurements were posted in three (3) conspicuous places and LGU Facebook account in lieu of website since there is no existing website yet.
observations.reason:
Written reply was received on March 12, 2021.

No similar audit observation has been noted in the current year’s audit.

observations.managementAction: The Ordinance localizing the applicability of RA 11313 otherwise known as the Safe Spaces Act has already passed the 1st and 2nd reading, and public hearing for the said ordinance was held on November 22, 2021.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe the provisions cited in LBC No. 84 dated April 13, 2007 in granting the revised RATA rates and be more prudent in releasing additional personnel benefits until such time the PS limitation had been observed. The Management granted RATA increase to boost the morale of the department heads and for humanitarian reasons. The LGU only exceeded the 55% PS limitation on the first year of implementation. On the succeeding years, the LGU is strictly observing and complying with the PS limitation and its rules and regulations.

Management is requesting for the list of Municipal officials who are obliged to refund the excess RATA with corresponding amount.
observations.reason:
The current year personal services expenditures are within the PS limitation. 

The personnel who received the benefits already separated from the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit an explanation on what have transpired during the process and make representation to the DSWD as to the proper action on this matter. observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accounting Office be directed to
determine if all these checks were indeed released to the proper payees.
Confirmation and/or notices should be sent to the respective payees/creditors so as
to verify the receipt of said checks and to inquire as to why these have not been
presented to the bank for payment.

Also, have stale checks still in the hands of the Treasurer, cancelled and
replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual,
Volume I and coordinate with the Accounting Office for proper recording in the
books. Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled
and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks. Henceforth, the Office of the
23
Municipal Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee at least one month
before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding
check.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to prepare and
submit the subsidiary schedules supporting the general ledger balances and to
maintain updated subsidiary ledgers and records of accounts duly supported with
complete documents to substantiate balances in the financial statements.

It is also recommended that management review its personnel complement
and ensure that offices are manned by sufficient and competent personnel to meet
deadlines and submit complete reports.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or her representatives conduct
the physical count of supplies and materials and reconcile the same with property
records which will then be reconciled with the accounting records. Further, we
recommend that the Municipal Treasurer or her representatives accomplish the
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued supported with proper documentation as
basis for the JEV that will be drawn to record the issuance from the stocks and
present accurately the balances of the inventory accounts in the financial
statements.

We recommend that management commence the process of disposal of
unserviceable properties in accordance with the guidelines set forth in COA
29
Circular No. 89-296 and make sure that the properties presented under PPE are
only those assets used in the operation of the LGU.

It is also recommended that management direct the Accountant to properly
classify the said deposits under receivership, for instance, Other Receivables or
other more appropriate accounts depending on the status of the legal action taken
and to take more concrete steps to fast-track recovery of said amount.

We recommend that the concerned Accountant/Bookkeeper be directed to
submit management direct the Accountant to submit the subject Bank
Reconciliation Statements together with the written explanation on the delayed
submission thereof.

We recommend that management direct the Bids and Awards Committee to
submit an explanation on why pre-procurement conferences were not conducted
and in regard to the other deficiencies noted in the procurement process and
supporting documents.

Thus, we recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive ensure
optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment and fast-
track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and
help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as
required under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 as amended
by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommend that the BAC expedite the procurement process of the banca
for the remaining engines to be utilized and distributed to the identified
beneficiaries. It is also recommended that management conduct inspection of the
distributed bancas to determine adherence to the terms and conditions of the
agreement and require periodic monitoring reports to be able to assess whether the
project’s objective is achieved.

We recommend that all personnel directly involved in the procurement
process should ensure that documentary requirements are filled-up pursuant to
COA Circular No. 92-386 and procurement transactions substantiated in
accordance with PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and RA 9184.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned
officials of the municipality submit a valid explanation on why the “pakyaw system”
was used in the procurement of services or rental of light and sound system instead
of public bidding.

We recommend that management submit a written explanation on why the
Annual Procurement Plan for 2017 is not consistent with the PPMPs and the budget
for the same period. Henceforth, preparation of APP should be consistent with the
annual budget and in accordance with RA No. 9184 and related issuances.

In view of the foregoing, we reiterate that Management:
a) Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to coordinate with the Municipal
School Board (MSB) and communicate with DepEd Santander District
and request for the immediate liquidation of cash advances granted to
DepEd officials and employees;
b) Require the MSB to authorize the municipal treasurer to disburse funds
from the SEF pursuant to the budget prepared and in accordance with
existing rules and regulations; and
c) Recommend for write-off all unsettled cash advances which had been in
the books for over ten (10) years in accordance with the guidelines set
forth under COA Circular 2016-005 on the Guidelines and Procedures on
the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash
Advances and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies (NGAs),
Local Government Units (LGUs) and Government-Owned and Controlled
Corporations (GOCCs).

We recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive appoint/designate
a new LDRRMO who can focus on the preparation of monthly and other required
reports in such form prescribed under COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002
and submit to the National DRRM Council through the Office of the Civil Defense
(OCD) and to the COA Auditor.

Henceforth, management should cause the suspension of payment of salaries
of officials/employees for failure on their part to submit the required documents and
reports until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.

We recommend that management submit justification/explanation on the
non-adherence to Section 15 of RA 9344 relative to the deficiency in allocation for
the strengthening and implementation of programs, projects and activities
advocating child rights and the non-utilization of the P50,000.00 allocation for
LCPC undertakings.

We recommend that management completely fill-up the mandated DRRM
positions to properly discharge the functions and responsibilities of the LDRRM
office including submission of required reports to different agencies and raise
people’s awareness on the adverse impact of disasters on population and economy.
Submit specified documentary requirements or a written explanation why such could not be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend constant follow-up with the Provincial Attorney as to the status of the claim for the money lost and that the Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution on how to recover the amount. The case filed against those responsible for the loss of the fund is already in progress.
observations.managementAction: Constant follow-up has been made though not in writing; the Provincial Attorney is in constant contact with the local government unit via telephone; the case filed against those responsible for the loss of the fund is already in progress.
observations.reason: The amount is still not recovered.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the municipal accountant coordinate with the Provincial Accounting Office for the copies of documents representing the donated materials and prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to take up the necessary adjusting entries in the municipal books of accounts; requisition/issue slips and summary of supplies and materials issued should support the JEV; and that municipal engineer and designated property officer should always coordinate with the municipal accountant for proper recording.
observations.managementAction: Management was not notified of the receipt of materials. The Municipal Accountant did not coordinate with the Cebu Provincial Accounting Office.
observations.reason: The materials were not coordinated with the Provincial Accounting Office and were not taken up in the municipal books.

observations.recommendation: Management should strictly observe the provisions cited in LBC No. 84 dated April 13, 2007 in granting the revised RATA rates and be more prudent in releasing additional personnel benefits until such time the PS limitation had been observed. The concerned officials should be required to refund the excess payment of their RATA.
observations.managementAction: The LGU exceeded the 55% PS limitation on the first year of implementation. On the succeeding years, the LGU is strictly observing and complying with the PS limitation and its rules and regulations. Management is requesting for the list of Municipal officials who are obliged to refund the excess RATA with corresponding amount.
observations.reason: No refund was made. The team is locating the vouchers, AOMs/AAR to be able to get the list of officials who are obliged to refund.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to look into the accounts payable which have been outstanding for more than two years and for which there are no valid claimants and to effect the necessary reversion of said accounts to the General Fund in conformity with R.A. 3526.
observations.managementAction: Serious efforts to locate necessary records/documents and to collect the receivables were not initiated; resolution to write-off the accounts is yet to be made.
observations.reason: No action taken to write-off the accounts; efforts to locate necessary records/documents were not initiated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management pursue the physical count of PPE and
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for
submission to the concerned auditor.

We recommend that management submit the above-specified documentary
requirements or a written explanation why such could not be submitted.

We, therefore, recommend that management submit a written explanation
on what has transpired that resulted to the BAC’s action of declaring the supplier as
the LCRB despite issues in the eligibility department. It is also recommended that
BAC members attend trainings on updates of RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommend that management submit the above-specified documents or a
written explanation why the same could not be submitted.

We, therefore, recommend that the Municipal Accountant affix her
signature/certification on the computations of the HRMO of the terminal leave
benefits of officials and employees to signify review made by the Accounting Unit
and that claims for terminal leave benefits be supported with the complete
documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommend that management submit the MOA between the LGU and
DOLE for the receipt of funds, Trust Agreement/MOA between the LGU and the
beneficiary association, project proposal or application for funding, accreditation
documents and other supporting documents to substantiate the receipt of funds and
implementation of said livelihood project.

We recommend that management comply with the pertinent provisions of
COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and 96-010 dated August 15,
1996 on the prompt submission of contracts and supporting documents, purchase
orders, work orders and notices of deliveries to ensure timely evaluation and
verification thereof.

We recommend that management direct the Accountant to submit the
complete set of financial statements prescribed in PPSAS to communicate
information about the municipality that is useful for accountability and for decision-
making purposes.

We recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive cause the prompt
submission of disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports
within the time frame provided by law, rules and regulations to avoid suspension or
disallowance in audit.

We recommend that the municipality prepare and submit/furnish a copy of
monthly reports in the prescribed forms required under COA Circular Nos. 2012-
002 and 2014-002 to the National DRRM Council through the Office of the Civil
Defense (OCD), and to the COA Auditor.

We recommend that Management facilitate the completion and approval of
the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan. Likewise, Management need to comply
with all applicable rules and regulations of the Ecological Solid Waste
Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment
by closing the open dump site, develop and operate a sanitary landfill as a final
disposal site for solid and eventually, residual wastes of the Municipality.
We recommend that management direct the Accountant to carefully analyze the Due to BIR account and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already prepared.
observations.reason: The Due to BIR account in the General Fund as of December 31, 2016 already showed a normal balance of P148,224.59. The negative balance in 2015 which was due to the remittance of taxes withheld in Trust Fund using the funds in General Fund was adjusted in January 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the specified documents to establish the validity of the claims.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were already submitted.
observations.reason: This office had already received the required documents such as the appointment of the OSCA head as Chairman of Senior Citizen’s Affairs by the agency head, authority to collect honoraria, plan/program of activities for senior citizens, accomplishment reports/certificate of completion of programmed activities, etc. which we found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant be directed to review, verify the reciprocal accounts from the subsidiary ledgers and source documents and effect the appropriate adjustments to correct the account balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have started to reconcile the two accounts.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the Due from Other Funds amounted to P1,161,368.70 while the Due to Other Funds was P1,162,118.70 or a difference of P750.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the immediate liquidation of the said cash advances; in case of refusal by the employees to liquidate or settle their accountabilities, direct the withholding of salaries to be applied in satisfaction of the unsettled cash advance pursuant to Section 37 of P.D. No. 1445; and defer clearances for retiring, resigning or transferring officials until they have liquidated their outstanding cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation for Advances to Officers and Employees amounted to P3,825,615.42 including cash advances for Intelligence Fund of P3,632,315.56. Liquidation for Intelligence Fund was already received by COA-ICFAU.
observations.reason: Demand letters were not sent to employees with long outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned Local Chief Executive ensure optimum utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment and fast-track development projects to provide immediate benefits to the constituents and help achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as required under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2005 as amended by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Management utilized the 20% DF for projects to be funded.
observations.reason: Out of 21 projects, only 11 implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a written explanation with justification and appropriate supporting documents for evaluation by COA technical personnel; otherwise, the variance shall be charged to the persons determined liable.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a written explanation; evaluation/justification from COA technical personnel is still awaited.
observations.reason: COA technical evaluation/justification for the variance of P157,429.15 is still not received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management seek a refund of the excess travel allowances from the concerned officials.
observations.managementAction: Partial refund was received by LGU.
observations.reason: ND# 2017-001-100(15) P50,249.00 received. The team is locating the vouchers, AOMs/AAR to be able to get the list of officials who are obliged to refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management utilize the LDRRMF for procurement of basic rescue and response equipment and to institutionalize arrangements and measures for reducing disaster risks.
observations.managementAction: Management utilized funds for slope protection project and procurement of basic rescue and response equipment.
observations.reason: Utilization of DRRM fund improved to 48%.

observations.recommendation: Communicate immediately with the Office of the President the reason for the non-implementation of the request and request for realignment or use of the funds.
observations.managementAction: LGU submitted a resolution to realign the funds because the LCE believes that the realignment will be more beneficial to the constituents. The request was denied by the Office of the President.
observations.reason: The request to realign the funds was denied by the Office of the President.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the concerned DepEd official be directed to settle/liquidate immediately in full the aforesaid cash advance as well as her other outstanding cash advances; discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to unauthorized officials/employees and adhere to COA Circular No. 97-002, R.A. 7160 and P.D. 1445; and enjoin the local school board to authorize the municipal treasurer to disburse funds from the SEF pursuant to the budget prepared and in accordance with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The DepEd Supervisor still acted as the DO for SEF.
observations.reason: Granting of special cash advances to the DepEd Supervisor was not discontinued.
